tool.title: Text Case Converter
tool.description: Convert text between uppercase, lowercase, title case, and more — instantly.
tool.placeholder: Type or paste your text here…

items.question: What is a text case converter?
items.answer: A text case converter is a free online tool that changes the capitalization of your text. You can convert between UPPER CASE, lower case, Title Case, Sentence case, Capitalized Case, aLtErNaTiNg CaSe, and iNVERSE CaSe with a single click.

items.question: How do I use this tool?
items.answer: Simply type or paste your text into the text area above, then click on one of the case mode tabs below it. Your text will be converted instantly — no need to press a submit button.

items.question: What is the difference between Title Case and Capitalized Case?
items.answer: Title Case follows AP/Chicago style rules, capitalizing most words but keeping short words like 'a', 'an', 'the', 'of', 'in', 'and', 'or', 'but', 'for', and 'nor' in lowercase (unless they start the sentence). Capitalized Case simply capitalizes the first letter of every word regardless.

items.question: What are Alternating and Inverse case?
items.answer: Alternating case (aLtErNaTiNg) switches each character between lowercase and uppercase, starting with lowercase. Inverse case flips every letter — uppercase becomes lowercase and vice versa, while numbers and symbols stay unchanged.

items.question: Is my text stored or sent to a server?
items.answer: No. All text conversion happens entirely in your browser using JavaScript. Your text is never sent to any server, making this tool completely private and safe to use with sensitive content.

seo.title: Text Case Converter — Free Online Tool
seo.description: Free online text case converter. Convert to UPPER CASE, lower case, Title Case, Sentence case, Capitalized, Alternating, and Inverse — instantly, no signup.

howto.name: How to Convert Text Case
howto.step1: Type or paste your text into the text area
howto.step2: Select a case mode tab (Sentence, lower, UPPER, Capitalized, Alternating, Title, or Inverse)
howto.step3: Your text converts instantly as you select the tab
howto.step4: Click the Copy button to copy the converted text to your clipboard

howItWorks.heading: How It Works
howItWorks.subheading: See how each text case mode transforms your text

sentence.name: Sentence case
sentence.description: Capitalize the first letter of each sentence while converting everything else to lowercase. Perfect for cleaning up messy text into readable paragraphs.

lower.name: lower case
lower.description: Convert all text to lowercase letters instantly. Ideal for normalizing data, creating CSS class names, or preparing text for case-sensitive comparisons.

upper.name: UPPER CASE
upper.description: Transform your entire text to uppercase letters online. Commonly used for headings, acronyms, emphasis, or formatting text for official documents.

capital.name: Capitalized Case
capital.description: Capitalize the first letter of every word in your text. Great for formatting names, titles, addresses, or any text where each word should start with a capital letter.

alternating.name: aLtErNaTiNg CaSe
alternating.description: Switch each character between lowercase and uppercase, starting with lowercase. Popular for memes, sarcastic text, and playful social media posts.

title.name: Title Case
title.description: Apply professional title capitalization following AP/Chicago style rules. Short words like 'a', 'the', 'of' stay lowercase unless they start the title.

inverse.name: iNVERSE CaSe
inverse.description: Flip the case of every letter — uppercase becomes lowercase and vice versa. Useful for creative text effects and reversing accidental caps lock typing.

tool.title: Reverse Text
tool.description: Reverse any text string instantly — see your text backwards in real time.
tool.placeholder: Type or paste your text here...

items.question: What does reverse text do?
items.answer: It takes your input text and reverses the order of all characters, so 'Hello' becomes 'olleH'.

items.question: Does it reverse words or characters?
items.answer: This tool reverses the entire string character by character. 'Hello World' becomes 'dlroW olleH'.

items.question: Can I reverse text with special characters?
items.answer: Yes. All characters including numbers, punctuation, and Unicode characters are reversed in order.

items.question: Is my text stored or sent to a server?
items.answer: No. All processing happens in your browser. Your text never leaves your device.

cards.example: "the quick brown fox" → "fox brown quick the"

cards.description: Reverse text generator popular for secret messages, backwards text social media posts, and fun username ideas. Works with Vietnamese, English, and special characters.
cards.example: "Xin chào Việt Nam" → "maN tệiV oàhc niX"

tool.title: Base64 Encode/Decode
tool.description: Encode text to Base64 or decode Base64 back to text — instantly.
tool.placeholder: Type or paste text to encode/decode...

items.question: What is Base64 encoding?
items.answer: Base64 is a way to represent binary data as a string of ASCII characters. It encodes every 3 bytes of data into 4 printable characters, making it safe to transmit binary data through text-based systems.

items.question: When should I use Base64 encoding?
items.answer: Base64 is commonly used to embed images in HTML/CSS (data URIs), encode binary data in JSON or XML, and transmit data through email systems or APIs that only support text.

items.question: Is Base64 the same as encryption?
items.answer: No. Base64 is encoding, not encryption. It does not protect your data — anyone can decode a Base64 string without a key. Do not use it to secure sensitive information.

items.question: Does this tool support Unicode characters?
items.answer: Yes. This tool uses the TextEncoder API to correctly handle Unicode text before encoding, so emojis and non-Latin characters are fully supported.

items.question: Is my text stored or sent to a server?
items.answer: No. All encoding and decoding happens entirely in your browser. Your text never leaves your device.

seo.title: Base64 Encode Decode — Free Online Tool
seo.description: Free online Base64 encoder and decoder. Encode text to Base64 or decode Base64 strings back to text — supports Unicode, no signup required.

howto.name: How to Encode or Decode Base64
howto.step1: Select the Encode or Decode tab
howto.step2: Type or paste your text into the input area
howto.step3: The result appears instantly in the output area below
howto.step4: Click Copy to copy the result to your clipboard

cards.title: Base64 Encode
cards.description: Convert text or data to Base64 encoded strings online — the standard encoding format used in email attachments, JWT tokens, data URLs, and API authentication headers.

cards.title: Base64 Decode
cards.description: Decode Base64 strings back to readable text instantly. Essential for developers debugging API responses, reading encoded cookies, or inspecting JWT payloads.

cards.title: Common Use Cases
cards.description: Base64 encoding is used in HTML image src (data:image/png;base64,...), CSS backgrounds, email MIME encoding, and storing binary data as text in JSON or XML.

tool.title: Slug Generator
tool.description: Generate URL-friendly slugs from any text, including Vietnamese.
tool.placeholder: Type or paste text to generate a slug...

items.question: What is a slug?
items.answer: A slug is the URL-friendly version of a page title. It uses only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens — no spaces or special characters. For example, 'Hello World' becomes 'hello-world'.

items.question: Does this tool support Vietnamese text?
items.answer: Yes. Vietnamese diacritics are transliterated to their ASCII equivalents. For example, 'Tiếng Việt' becomes 'tieng-viet', making it ideal for creating clean URLs from Vietnamese content.

items.question: What happens to special characters?
items.answer: Apostrophes and similar word-internal punctuation are removed without replacement. Other special characters and spaces are converted to hyphens. Multiple consecutive hyphens are collapsed into one.

items.question: Why are hyphens better than underscores in URLs?
items.answer: Google recommends hyphens over underscores as word separators in URLs. Search engines treat hyphens as word boundaries, which can improve the readability and ranking of your URLs.

items.question: Is my text stored or sent to a server?
items.answer: No. All slug generation happens in your browser. Your text never leaves your device.

seo.title: Slug Generator — Free Online URL Slug Tool
seo.description: Free online slug generator. Convert any text — including Vietnamese — to URL-friendly slugs instantly. No signup required.

howto.name: How to Generate a URL Slug
howto.step1: Type or paste your text into the input area
howto.step2: The URL slug is generated instantly as you type
howto.step3: Click Copy to copy the slug to your clipboard

cards.title: URL-Friendly Slugs
cards.description: A slug is the readable part of a URL after the domain. Our slug generator converts any text to lowercase, replaces spaces with hyphens, and removes special characters for clean SEO-friendly URLs.

cards.title: Vietnamese Support
cards.description: Full Vietnamese diacritics support — automatically converts ắ, ề, ổ, ượ and all Vietnamese characters to their ASCII equivalents. Perfect for Vietnamese bloggers and developers.

cards.title: SEO Best Practices
cards.description: Search engines prefer short, descriptive slugs with keywords. This tool follows slug best practices: all lowercase, hyphens not underscores, no stop words, max 60 characters recommended.
cards.example: "How to Convert Text Case Online Free" → "how-to-convert-text-case-online-free"

tool.title: Password Generator
tool.description: Generate secure random passwords with customizable length and character types.

items.question: How secure is the generated password?
items.answer: Passwords are generated using the Web Crypto API (crypto.getRandomValues), which provides cryptographically secure random numbers. A 16-character password using all character types has over 95 bits of entropy, far beyond what can be brute-forced.

items.question: Is my password stored anywhere?
items.answer: No. Passwords are generated entirely in your browser and never sent to any server. Once you close or refresh the page, the password is gone.

items.question: What symbols are included?
items.answer: The symbols set includes: ! @ # $ % ^ & * ( ) _ + - = [ ] { } | ; : , . < > ? These are widely accepted by most websites and password managers.

items.question: What password length do you recommend?
items.answer: We recommend at least 16 characters with a mix of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols. For critical accounts such as email or banking, use 20 or more characters.

items.question: Can I use this for passphrases?
items.answer: This tool generates random character passwords, not passphrases. For passphrases, you would need a word-based generator. Both approaches are secure when the output is long enough.

seo.title: Password Generator — Free Secure Online Tool
seo.description: Free secure password generator. Create strong random passwords with custom length and character types using cryptographically secure randomness. No signup required.

howto.name: How to Generate a Secure Password
howto.step1: Set your desired password length using the slider
howto.step2: Toggle the character types you want: uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols
howto.step3: A secure password is generated automatically
howto.step4: Click Regenerate for a new password, or Copy to save the current one

howItWorks.heading: How to Create a Strong Password

cards.title: Random Password Generation
cards.description: Generate cryptographically random passwords using uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols. Strong random passwords are impossible to guess and protect against brute-force attacks.

cards.title: Password Length & Strength
cards.description: Security experts recommend passwords of at least 16 characters. Each additional character exponentially increases the number of possible combinations, making your password stronger.
cards.example: 8 chars = 645 trillion combos · 16 chars = 62 trillion trillion

cards.title: Password Security Tips
cards.description: Never reuse passwords across sites. Use a password manager to store unique passwords for each account. Enable two-factor authentication (2FA) for critical accounts like email and banking.
cards.example: Use different passwords for Gmail, Facebook, banking, shopping
